Description: Ant characters are strong, physically and mentally, they are very social and communicative, believing in teamwork, loyalty and duty. Their only failing is perhaps that they are overly confident and this can lead them to bite off far more than they can chew in some situations. Ants are so social that many are capable of swarm forms, controlling multiple bodies at once, although such control is often limited in either number of forms or precision of control, large swarms are difficult to coordinate well.

Antelope characters are graceful and athletic, they are quick thinkers, and unfortunately are quick to jump to assumptions. They can seem paranoid to some people, always assuming the worst of any situation, and then running in terror, but often this will keep them safe. Antelope Souls often develop the ability to leap from one Lea path to another, throwing themselves across the Lea almost instantly. This ability can be further developed into an abilty to teleport instantly through the Lea about as far as they can normally jump.

Ape characters are sometimes split into two types, quiet, peaceful, gentle intelligences and raucous, violent, strong beasts, but the truth is they are both. While they are thought of as vegetarian, only a few apes will turn down meat when they can get it, and most eat insects from time to time. They can even be patient, planning and brutal, striking in deadly ambushes and rampaging highly coordinated hunting parties.

Armadillo characters are seen as trust-worthy, peaceful and compassionate, and unless you are an insect this is probably true. They are a predator who look and act more like prey, and often have more in common with Field Bulmäs than their Followers brethren. Armadillo Souls often develop an ability to create a barrier or boundary effect around themselves that can protect them from most spiritual and a lot of phsyical attacks.

Badger characters known as quiet and unassuming sorts, they generally keep to themselves, although they can be quite curious from time to time, they are usually too wise to draw attention to their investigations. Although badgers keep to themselves and are generally quiet, do not assume they are defenceless, as Badgers are strong, tenacious and quite dangerous to animals even as large as a wolf, or a bear. Badger Souls are known amongst the Bulmäs as magicians, capable of manipulating the Lea in strange ways that mimic, and some say improve on, Færy magics.

Bat characters are one part playful trickster, and another part brave hunter, they can be predator or prey, depending upon the ecosystem and fruit or insect eating as well. Bats are perceptive and cunning, they keep to the darkest shadows and strike suddenly when they attack, often with theatrical or playful flourishes to their plans. They are highly social, but can be manipulative, usually though these manipulations are simply the set-up to some joke or surprise. Bats can occasionally turn nasty, though, and if this happens their theatrics, tricks and manipulations will also become dark and nasty. Bat Souls are known for their ability to perceive other souls, reading them at range as most would taste them, and their ability to tie on souls they taste quickly and precisely, as though they had eaten them. Souls they eat, they often learn secrets from.

Bear characters are notable for their strength, patience and aggressive nature, although this can just be their protective nature getting out of hand. Bears, whether actual bears or humans with bear-like qualities are often nurturing, but authoritative figures, commanding respect by their presence alone. Bears have a nature of duality due to their hibernation through the winter, and are regarded as a symbol of devoted motherhood and berserk rage. Bear Souls are known for their ability to sleep in the land, although less capable than Lord Vermis, who can sleep for millenia, Bears can effectively hide their souls amongst all living things, including plants while they hibernate through the winter. Although they sleep, they dream the events their territory experiences.

Beetle characters are amongst the most common amongst the Bulmäs, they are known for their pleasant, sympathetic, and exceptionally optimistic natures. Beetles are considered gifted, although this gift varies, some are perceptive (seeing things others cannot see), some empathic (feeling what others feel around them), others are creative (artists, smiths, and musicians), some are simply beautiful to behold and a final few are gifted magically (able to manipulate luck, or create order from chaos), but they are rarely known for their leadership or intelligence, but are incredible team-workers.

Bull characters are known for their incredible strength, toughness, stamina and determination. Bull-like humans and the animals alike are known for their stubbornness and resilience. Bull Souls are among the more aggressive of the herbivores, and they are known to employ weapons as part of their defence of their Field. Bull Souls will often carry paired Horn-blades, sharpen their own horns, or may own a Moon-stave weapon.

Butterfly characters are seen as beautiful, creative and communicative and joyful souls, often idealistic, and usually optimistic. They are associated with transformations, and many butterfly’s pass through a much darker, depressive phase when younger, sometimes this can be due to a past-life trauma that must be worked around, other times it is about the butterfly discovering something, and realising they can love this discovered part of themselves. Butterfly Souls are known amongst the Bulmäs as the most powerful Lea walkers able to create huge Lea Gates that travel immense distances through the Lea, Butterfly Souls have no problem opening Portals into, or out of, the Lea, in fact, they can find they have difficulty passing into the Lea without bursting open a portal whenever they try.

Carnivore characters are generic predatory creatures that are not specified elsewhere. They are generally solitary, aggressive, proud, and while never completely stupid they always think they are smarter than they actually are, of course a few are exceptionally smart. Carnivore characters usually have a nomadic lifestyle following prey as they migrate, although some favour a territorial approach as a carnivorous Broccen.

Cat characters have an uncommon combination of traits. Cat character is perceptive, wise and intelligent, often to the point of it being uncanny, and hedonistic, seeking physical pleasures where ever they can find them, but they are also introverted and solitary usually, preferring to remove themselves from crowds, as most people irritate them, as they see them as stupid, or unaware. Cats can often seem aloof, unapproachable, but if they choose you as an ally, they can be a great help. Cats, especially the big cats, have a reputation as cold-blooded killers that toy with their kills, this toying is the way the cats practise and perfect their killing techniques. Cat Souls are known to develop very individualistic abilities that can seem magickal, such as their Soul Sight, Soul Whiskers, and

Centaur characters are very rare, they are known for their incredible knowledge and wisdom, because Centaurs often have incredible memories that can reach back through many incarnations perfectly. Centaurs are the doctors, smiths, theologians, scientists and teachers of the Bulmäs, as well as strong fighters and proficient hunters, this is on top of their beauty and grace which will often let them pass as horses, pterippi or unicorns.

Centipede characters are known for their swiftness, aggression and ferocity. Everything about them is fast and deadly, they are quick to anger, flying into a furious, blinding rage, where they usually throw themselves at their enemy, ignoring everything to attack. Centipedes are so aggressive, and so powerful that even the Vermis consider them a threat. They are anti-social, usually solitary, although they do enjoy some gatherings, especially if they can demonstrate their physical prowess.

Cephalopod (squid, octopus, and cuttlefish) characters are intelligent, curious, changeable, and somewhat mischievous, they are the most mercurial tricksters of the oceans. Cephalopod characters are used to having a sub-brain that controls each limb, and this can allow them to walk multiple lea paths simultaneously, creating multiple bodies in potentially separate locations that are still mentally connected. The most a Cephalopod can do this is equal to their Incarna Score.

Chimera characters created by a blend of other animal souls together, a few chimerical types are considered as distinct types. Chimera souls can be considered monstrous by some Bulmäs, but in general they just blend the attributes of their various component souls.

Cow characters are social, calm, grounded individuals. Real cows or cow-like people are usually considered pleasant, if not exciting or clever. They may be hard working, and create an abundance, but are usually considered dull, however do not confuse dullness for safe, cow’s are almost as strong as bulls.

Crab characters are usually considered selfish and overly emotional, as well as defensive, protective and, of course, crabby. However, the thing about crabs is, they are incredibly adaptable and complex forming surprisingly large social groups. Their body shape has evolved multiple times, as a strong example of convergent evolution, or Bulmäs walking something new onto a crab path.

Crocodile (and the Alligator and Caiman) characters are liminal characters, often displaying dual natures, that seem oxymoronic or paradoxical, such as being cold-hearted, but emotionally nurturing, or being incredibly patient, but short-tempered. In general, Crocodiles are known for their wisdom, strength, stamina and patience, a crocodile will lay plans months, or years, ahead of time, with an attention to detail that can seem like foresight to others. In fact, it is said that some crocodiles, when appearing to sleep underwater are actually swimming forward in time to see glimpses of the future.

Crow characters are seen as mischievous tricksters. Intelligent, flexible, adaptable, and audacious, crows have a reputation for magic, with crows being interested in prestidigitation, pick-pocketing, illusions, transformations and doom-weaving, although their luck learning such skills will vary. Crows are warm and social beings happy to spend time in the company of almost any other beings, and almost completely fearless, although that will get them into trouble from time to time, crows know it can also lead to interesting, and useful friendships.

Deer characters are graceful, alert and while they are often seen as quite shy, quick to flee any situation that becomes too tense or dangerous, they are actually remarkably social and perceptive, and love hanging out in crowds, playing games with friends, dancing, eating and drinking, but are excellent at reading cues around them, which means when trouble begins most deer are already gone. Deer can be actual animals or just good-looking, healthy, but slightly anxious people.

Dinosaur Characters cover a wide number of different dinosaur species, these are usually large animals that combine lizard, mammal and bird body-parts in weird combinations. Dinosaurs might have horns, beaks, armoured bodies, huge frills of feathers or bone, spiked or clubbed tails, some favour two legs, others four, many swapping between. They are generally seen friendly social beings, not given to academic or intellectual pursuits, unless it is history, but with a rustic folk-wisdom. Some can be belligerent, even aggressive, but this is a protective bluster, and if reassured they will quickly stand-down from battle, but if challenged they fight with deadly weapons to dissuade, injure or kill an attacker.

Dog characters are loyal, devoted, brave and trusting, whether a real dog, or a trusted friend, they are great Allies. Dogs are tenacious and unwilling to compromise, which can mean that if their loyalty or trust is abused, they can twist into an angry, dangerous and destructive foe.

Dolphin characters are often seen as fun-loving, social, and graceful creatures, full of joy at everything, and ultimately a symbol of freedom, however this is not the full story of dolphin, while they are fun-loving, it is often bordering on hedonism, they are social, but only within their small cliques or pods, and while they are graceful, it can be a brutal grace. Dolphins are an embodiment of freedom, but that also means they are free to do as they will, often without consequences falling upon them, and egged on by their friends this freedom and hedonism can become selfishness and abuse.

Donkey characters are perceived as strong, capable and long-suffering, they are friendly and range from wise to comically stupid in nature, but even the wise are slow, plodding thinkers. Donkeys are not as quick or as powerful as horses, but they are stalwart companions and often braver and more loyal than horses alone, and can have a calming effect on horses.

Dragonfly characters are known as ferocious carnivores, with lightning fast reflexes and incredible vision. For a while all the Dragonfly souls were bound to Vermis, but increasingly since Teucoi walked the all-path more Dragonflies have appeared among the Hunting parties. Dragonfly Bulmäs are notable as having no sense of smell, nor any ability to process smells in other body forms, but they compensate by processing visual and sound information much more quickly than anyone else, always granting them an automatic success on any speed or reaction-time based Tests, including Ordeal initiative.

Duck characters are known for their incredible stamina, although they are not the smartest, occasionally confusing themselves and others as they try to communicate too much too quickly, they are very adaptable, loving and friendly folk, quickly adapting to new situations.

Eagle characters are known for their nobility, majesty and power. They are often perceptive or even visionary, and others look to them for inspiration and leadership automatically, but they can be arrogant, aloof and overbearing.

Elephant characters are known for their size, strength and memory, they are recognised for their wisdom and ability to remove barriers also. Their strength can be physical, mental, spiritual, or even supernatural in nature.

Fish characters are social with their own kind, but can be aloof or timid around anything strange or unknown. Fish characters are generally emotionally reserved, and can seem cool or even cold to most people, but they are completely open with those closest to them.

Flea characters are cowardly, quick to leave at the slightest sign of danger, but are determined, they will return as soon as their fear subsides. Fleas are selfish, social as far as it is getting them something, but quick to abandon those around them for a better situation if it is presented. Flea characters are perceptive and sensitive to changes in their environment that others may not notice, and fleas can tell as lot about something from a taste, even learning from the blood they drink. This can make fleas very knowledgeable and even capable of magical acts.

Fowl characters (whether chicken, peafowl, partridge, or turkey) are known as determined, enthusiastic, and somewhat proud beings, they are social beings, mixing often with others. Fowl characters are known to be balanced in their approach to life, they are bold to a point, but know when to take flight, and they often have a knack for lateral thinking, and find new uses for old things. Fowl characters are often noted for having a habit of uncovering the truth.

Fox characters are known to be cunning, perceptive, wise and of a protective nature, playful and fun-loving, but they are also known to be greedy, wantonly destructive and hedonistic. Foxes are nocturnal hunters that hide and play by day, but they are also strong tunnelers and are many are known to dig tunnels through the Lea that can allow them to view a few days ahead or behind themselves in a given area. This adds to their trickster nature allowing them to retro-actively set up traps and escape routes that can seem magical.

Frog characters are known as being incredibly social, talkative and noisy individuals, they are considered dual-natured by some, because of their amphibious lifestyle, and are associated with a knowledge of poisons and medicines, that they may use capriciously to delude, confuse and harm, as well as cure illnesses, aid and even renew others. Frogs are often prosperous and associated with fertility, some can even control the weather to benefit the lands they live in.

Giraffe characters are seen as aloof and graceful, and it is true that they are peaceful and have a protective nature, and giraffes are known for their, at times, clairvoyantly perceptive foresight, but their alleged aloofness is more often shyness, and their grace while present is required, without grace a giraffe is comically clumsy.

Goat characters are known for their persistence, determination, greed, and general fearlessness. Goat characters can often appear foolish, as their bravado and stubbornness will get them into a lot more trouble than it gets them out of, however goats know this about themselves and will often have some back-up plan or have made some provision to get themselves out of the trouble they get into. Goats are intrepid, and industrious, leaping where few other beings dare to tread, always pushing and breaking through barriers to find new exploitable fields.

Griffin characters are seen as regal, majestic, and guardian beings, they are powerful predators, marked by their highly perceptive nature, valorous bravery and deadly sharp claws and beak, but are also highly territorial, this is due to their often Broccen like nature. They are the only predatory Bulmäs who claim Broccen territories apart from Vermis. Some Bulmäs believe that Griffin souls were actually created by the Broccen using their chimerical abilities, to act as guardians around Broccen territories.

Hawk characters are focused, competitive and often aggressive. Whether a human being with a particularly war-like attitude, or an actual bird, hawks are energetic, protective and strategic thinkers, as well as predatory. They will strike at an enemy’s weaknesses, ideally without warning, and will not relent or waiver. The hawk’s singular focus can be a weakness, as it can cause it to overlook something it should easily have seen.

Hedgehog characters are seen as gentle and protective, curious but timid, but many also forget that they are predators, as well as prey, and in fact they could be better described as cool and collected rather than gentle. Above all else, hedgehog characters are practical, they are resourceful and intelligent enough to avoid trouble.

Herbivore characters are generic prey animals that are not specified elsewhere. Generally Herbivore characters are social, affable, friendly, but can be shy or timid especially around strangers or in unfamiliar situations. Prey characters can be smart or dim, but universally believe that others have worth, and are usually humble about their own abilities. Herbivore animals are usually nomadic, following seasonal feeding patterns and dietary changes, but a few are territorial and may behave like vegetarian Broccen.

Hippopotamus characters are known for their emotional turmoil and aggressive nature. Hippos are liminal, amphibious beings and this gives them a dual nature. They are strong and authoritative, intuitive and deep, focused and reserved, but they are also stubborn and judgemental beings, that can become reckless and impulsive as well as aggressive, Hippos are emotionally turbulent, usually dealing with complex inner conflicts that they do not wish public.

Hominid characters are rare amongst the Bulmäs, only a very few souls will claim descent from the man-like apes, most would rather claim Ape descent when possible. Hominid characters are seen as sly, duplicitous, cunning and terrifying, they are actually curious and usually peaceful beings, but their knowledge, intelligence and creative reasoning often grants them technologies that grant them an edge over all other predators and prey. Among the Hominid souls, for example, there are those who favour controlling and manipulating their environment, which can seem dark and dangerous to other Bulmäs, especially when these technologies are magical in nature.

Horse characters are patient and strong, noble and often beautiful to look upon. Whether an actual horse, or just a horse-like companion matters little. The horse character, however, can be flighty, easily distracted, and may even be a coward, despite their strengths.

Hyena characters are known for their trickster ways, coarse dark humoured giggling, gender ambiguity, and brutality. Hyenas are scavengers first, and as such closely associate with vultures and Vermis, although they never were folded into Vermis in the same way as some other souls, hyenas were always too tricksy to be caught that way. Hyena characters are strong strategists and tactical thinkers, with excellent communication and survival skills.

Insect characters are the most common of all the Bulmäs, although huge numbers of their souls became trapped as aspects of Lord Vermis until Teucoi completed the all-path. Insect characters are usually fairly social (some especially so), with very varied temperaments ranging from timid to bold, some are stealthy assassins, others exuberant, iridescent dandies and completely silent to deafeningly chatty. The only thing that unites all insects is that they are never very intelligent.

Lemur characters are known for their sense of humour and generally good-tempered nature, they often want to be the best they can be at things, having a competitive and driven personality, but can have unrealistic expectations based on over-confidence and lack of understanding. Lemur characters have a natural dexterity and coordination and they can seem very like squirrel characters, but Lemurs are more attuned to night life than squirrels and are less industrious or well prepared. Where the squirrel will work hard to make time to play hard, the lemur will play hard first, and then muddle through in the end.

Lions characters are similar to other cats, having similar carnivorous tendencies, but lions are different in one major regard, to all the other cat types. Lions are incredibly, and deeply social, they do not share most cats solitary and distant nature. Instead, the lion radiates majesty, charisma and cooperation, because of this social nature and physical prowess the Lion is almost free of fear, only fearing the largest packs, and animals.

Lizard characters are cool and alert, unblinkingly aware of what is around them, and quick to move on if things are not going their way. Lizards are believed to be wise and knowledgeable, and some are, some have knowledge of medicine and poisons, but others are gifted with knowledge of secret ways, or very ancient survival knowledge. Lizard characters are often shown to have incredible powers of regeneration, some are able to even shed their body-parts to leave behind for predators to attack, while the lizard escapes and regrows their tail.

Louse characters are thought to be similar to fleas, although in fact this is not strictly true, as the louse is less cowardly than the flea, but generally prefers to keep a lower profile and not draw attention to themselves. Lice characters simply take from others, ideally without being sensed or noticed, they do not understand that others are offended or upset by this behaviour as the louse is usually a solitary and anti-social being. Louse characters are unlikely to develop the ability to learn from blood drinking, as they are simply too self-absorbed to care about their food, beyond it keeping them alive.

Minotaur characters (such as Asterion) are known for their stamina, imperviousness and near immortality, which they were originally given as part of their role as the Guardians of the Labyrinth and the Bodyguards of the Goddess. It is said that their blood will not spill, and that even if their body is killed and destroyed they will reform around their soul by the next dawn, and this is certainly true of Labyrinthine Bulmäs. Minotaur characters are taciturn and stoic to the point of asocial silence, although some are very good listeners, and communicate well with body language, few have ever heard a Minotaur speak. Minotaur souls are often confused with Bull souls with whom they of course share many traits.

Mole characters are sensitive, tactile people, keen listeners and obsessed with touching, smelling and tasting things, especially rich or fine things, but this is more their sensual nature rather than some pride or love of the finer things in life. Mole characters are known for their strong sense of self, and strong faith (usually in others, or religious in nature), and they are also known for their knowledge of roots and tunnels. Mole characters have an understanding of the deep lea that surpasses even Labyrinthine Bulmäs, as no Mole soul has ever got lost into the labyrinth, and they are believed to have uncanny ways of sensing and avoiding the Increated in the deep Lea, although they cannot fight them.

Monkey characters are seen as playful, fun-loving, often trickster creatures, that are intelligent and resourceful, associated with art, balance and harmony. Monkeys can be faithful and penitent, or lust-filled and debauched, and anything in between.

Moose (or Elk) Characters are known for their strength and majesty, although they can be graceful or clumsy in almost equal measure. Moose are usually very grounded personalities, down to earth and not prone to flights of fancy, or even excessive speculation, but they always seem to know the right thing to say to make another laugh or smile. Moose characters are strong and resilient, but have a soft side and a kind-heart that they must keep protected and secret, or it will be easily exploited by others.

A Moth character is graceful and beautiful, but are always somehow distant or strange. They are usually asocial, and are great navigators, having some of the longest lea paths, that sweep down through the Labyrinth. They have a reputation for both silence and illusions, although in most cases these are exaggerations.

A Mouse character is timid, but sneaky. They are quick to flee conflict of any sort, but what they cannot take by force they know they can take by theft. Unlike Rats, mice are not as social, they are too timid to form large groups. Mice are not smarter than Rats, but they are better thieves, more attuned to small details and more perceptive than their ratty brethren, mice are naturally dismissed and overlooked by all but a few specialised predators.

Otter characters are curious and playful, to the point that they can seem entirely child-like with their love of play. That is until the game becomes rougher, with otters it is all fun and games until someone loses a finger, or a life. Otters play hard, work hard and fight hard, and do all three with the same exuberance and sense of fun, throwing themselves completely into whatever they are currently doing one hundred percent. Otters find it harder to plan ahead or dwell on the past than other souls, in fact many otters have a hard time walking their own soul-paths back, they would rather revel in the immediacy of now rather than think about their own history or past lives.

Owl characters are considered very intelligent and intellectual by others, they are often philosophical thinkers, and usually have knowledge of occult and fringe topics. Owls have a reputation for laziness, but this is due to their nocturnal nature, they work hardest when no one is watching. Owl characters are known as deadly hunters, but they are careful, never placing themselves in unnecessary danger, and always scouting around a target before they attack. An owl would rather leave prey to escape than risk a trap.

Parrot characters are gregarious, charming and quirky characters that have a deep confidence and intelligence. Parrot characters are natural risk-takers, jumping in, or speaking up before they have thought if it is appropriate.

Phoenix characters are noted for their self-sacrificing and noble natures. Phoenix characters upon gaining their travelling soul will also experience an intense fever, but those who survive are always reborn. They will gain access to Bulmäs Phoenix flame magic, which can both burn and heal as the phoenix wishes, allowing a phoenix to cloak themselves in a protective, healing flame, that will burn their enemies.

Pigs are often thought of as a simple character, boorish, violent, and greedy. However, while it is true that a pig character is hedonistic and does love their food and drink, and they are quick to anger, and when angered are merciless and capable of anything, they are also loyal and brave, and usually quite cheerful sorts. While not generally considered smart, they have an instinct for sniffing out secrets, and can often spot and realise things that "smarter" people miss. Of course, some pigs use their gifts to persuade and manipulate the gullible and naive into positions the pig can take advantage of too.

Porcupine characters are kind, humble, independent, determined and very grounded, but Porcupine characters are often overly sensitive, critical of others, give off a sense of being unapproachable, or even hostile. They are accused of being angry quite often, and this is not always true, but Porcupines who do lose their temper lose it completely.

Pterippi characters (also known as winged horses, or Pegasi after Pegasus) are exceptionally rare souls, who were tasked as messengers of the goddess. Pterippi are known for their grace, speed and beauty, similar to horses, for whom they are often mistaken, are also known for their imaginations, and ecstatic visions, which often contain traces of prophesy and spiritual and divine magics. Pterippi are able to move through the lea up to a hundred times faster than other Bulmäs, but whenever they do so, regardless of the path they take they will arrive in their soul-form.

Pterosaur characters are known for their huge wings and often having a large brightly coloured crest on their head. Pterosaur characters are excitable types, fairly social, with strong senses and a wide variety of diets, they are easily confused with birds and bats, but are distinct and pre-date both by a long time. In fact, both birds and bats were probably created by walking mammals and theropods along Pterosaur paths. Pterosaur characters are often considered the most versatile fliers, even more so than bird types as they have millions of years more practice.

A Rabbit (or Hare) character is noted by their love of comfort, security, and family. Whether the actual animal or a human who is rabbit-like they are also timid, quiet, shy beings, but are noted for their speed and their ability to burrow deeper for additional security. Rabbit characters are generally pleasant and charming, but turn vicious when cornered, and reveal hidden strengths and weapons.

Rat characters are resourceful, clever and sneaky. It applies to both the animal and humans who are rat-like that they are social, fast and nimble, able to get themselves into, and out of, situations that others would not contemplate. Rats can be successful, and generous, sharing their abundances with everyone, just don’t ask too deeply about how the rat came to have such an abundance, rats think its yours if you take it. Rats have a reputation for duplicity, this stems from their misunderstood nature, a rat doesn’t hold steadfast beliefs, they are not dogmatic or loyal, but independent thinkers who agree with whoever is currently making the best case, and will turn on anyone that threatens them.

Raven characters are mysterious, mindful and mischievous, although occasionally melancholy and macabre. Whether a bird or a dark cloaked person, the Raven has an almost bi-polar personality, they can be a laughing trickster one minute, before lapsing into despair, or dwell on deep matters for days, before taking a break to prank another, the only steadiness of their personality is their quick, mercurial, creative, intelligence. Ravens often fill the role of messengers, whether delivering gossip, or as a harbinger of sorrows. They are also known as battle-birds, as they gather over battlefields, but while they’re not overly aggressive they are highly territorial and opportunistic.

Rhinoceros characters are seen as sturdy, strong, and resilient, they are known for their amazing stamina, and short-sightedness (which is as much mental as a vision problem). Rhinos are generally peaceful characters, but they are quick to anger, preferring to charge an enemy at least once before leaving, just so they know the rhinoceros is serious.

Sauropod characters are based on those huge long-necked and long-tailed dinosaurs, they are also known as Thunder-lizards by the Bulmäs. Sauropod characters are known for their strength, perpetual hunger and their slowness, they think and move slowly just because of their enormous size, the only parts of their body that moves with any sort of speed are their neck, moving their head to new vegetation, and tail which moves constantly to counter-balance the head and body when walking, and can also be used as an enormous bull-whip, accelerating the tip beyond the speed of sound, lacerating enemies and creating a incredibly loud crack that can stun predators and act as a warning to others in the herd.

Scorpion characters are known for their secretive, perceptive nature, they can have an assertive and charismatic personality, that draws people in, before the scorpion ambushes them. Scorpion characters have a gift for poison and medicine, and has a strong association with death, with many believing Scorpions are a form of Vermis. This is because scorpions, unlike most predators, favour eating other predators, with some hunting other scorpions, spiders, or centipedes as their preferred prey, they hunt by tracking scent trails across the ground and detecting vibrations in the air, generally launching a sudden ambush upon their target after hiding themselves somehow.

Seal characters are known for their imaginations and creativity, they love learning new tricks and skills, this can be mistaken for a playful, or flippant personality, but the truth is seals are joyful, happy beings, but they are just as happy killing, smashing, and destroying as they are doing anything. Seals enjoy life and get a kick out of evading another predator with a cool trick, especially if it means they get a kill of their own.

Shark characters are known for their strength, power and authority. Shark characters are instinctual hunters, ruthless and furious, but they are careful too, never risking injury for a meal. Sharks are curious beings, but mainly wonder what something will taste like. Sharks are always on the move, tirelessly swimming, and this means they can find it impossible to settle in a territory, even as a Broccen.

Skunk characters are seen as offensive, foul and obscene, but they are also usually non-violent and self-protecting beings. Skunk characters are considered brave, but it is simply that their fear response is hard-coded as fight then flight, where fight ideally involves spraying foul-smelling  musk at the enemy as an attack.

Snake characters are cool, patient and manipulative, they like to get their own way and always have their own goals, but snakes are slow to anger, preferring to bide their time and wait for a chance to strike back later, although once a snake loses their cool they will become deadly and violent, not caring who they hurt to get what they want.

Songbird characters are easy to understand as they are always singing, and their songs will reflect their mood. Songbirds wear their heart on their sleeve and they are always musical, but they range from cheerful three-note whistlers, to brooding poet types, with complex melodic, lyrical and harmonic patterns to their songs.

Spider characters are seen as quiet, wise types, creative and gifted in knowledge especially esoterica that includes poison and magic. Spider characters can learn to manipulate the lea as a web, allowing them to claim territory and even in some cases doom-bend.

Squirrel characters are known for their preparedness, hoarding, balance and agility. Whether an actual squirrel or somebody who seems squirrel-like, they are industrious, but also playful, putting equal energy into fun and work is the squirrel way, but no matter how well-prepared a squirrel may be, don’t expect them to help out much in a conflict. The squirrel is more likely to avoid and placate than confront.

Theropod characters (which are the three-toed, two-legged, long-tailed proto-birds of the dinosaurs) are seen as aggressive brutes by some, but they are quite social, adaptable and intelligent hunters. They are very perceptive, but often impatient and hungry, and this can lead them into trouble, as they are drawn so strongly to the scents of blood and carrion, and by the sounds of struggle and pain, that they can blunder into traps such as pits very easily. Theropod characters always have a number of deadly weapons at their disposal, and strong tactical knowledge, but they favour flanking assaults and surprise charges.

Toad characters are considered disgusting by many, often seen as dirty, fawning, slimy, sleazy, compliant, gluttonous, cowardly and impure. This is often true more of human toads, than the actual animal. However, the Toad has redeeming qualities, a certain wisdom, as they always know their own mind, foibles and weaknesses, they are never delusional about themselves, their position, or how others view them. They also know how to hide and submerge themselves in their environment, as well as how to flatter and insult others, and these manipulations can grant them power of those who think them subservient.

Tortoises, or their semi or fully aquatic cousins, terrapins or turtles are known for their slowness of action and generally peaceful nature. Because they are physically slow, the tortoise is usually a wise tactician and planner, thinking ahead will save them unnecessary trips, and while generally placid, a tortoise will bite if left with no other recourse. Tortoises are known for their defences, and no tortoise will ever be found without at least one backup plan.

Unicorn characters are very rare, they were never common, being the bridge between the Fæ and the Bulmäs. It is said that Unicorn souls can manipulate the Lea with their horns like the Færies manipulate the threads of the Tapestry. Unicorns are often arrogant, self-possessed beings, they believe in their own superiority in a way some would find alarming. Unicorns are famously wild and ferocious, although this aggression is overstated in some cases, they are a prey animal with a carnivore’s predilection for attack.

Vermis is bound by a great duty and overall sense of weariness, that only seems to lift when they travel to a new world. Vermis characters are all technically a singular character, Lord Vermis, who is tasked to collect the souls of the Bulmäs who die, and shepherd those souls to new worlds where they can be reincarnated once again. Vermis has an over-riding sense of duty, the blended instincts of worms, maggots, flies and dragons, a hive-mind / gestalt / swarm experience that believes itself devoid of any individuality, a desire to be solemn and careful with the souls harboured, burning hunger for Bulmäs souls (especially those that have "Dead" or "Dying" Hitches of any type), and a considerable amount of frustration and anger at their lot. Each Vermis character will display a slightly different amount of each of these traits, based on which parts their original personality most resembled, and since the All-Walkers this effect has increased with individual Vermis varying more.

Vulture characters are known for their patience and incredibly keen senses. Vulture characters are known as death birds and have no fear of Vermis, who owned their form for quite a while until Teucoi of the All-path released several Vulture souls. Vulture characters are seen as serious, perceptive, and trustworthy, but are also feared for their links with death, and are seen as aggressive, unclean and disease-ridden, although this last is rarely true as vultures are virtually immune to all bacteria and most viruses.

Warthog characters are seen as courageous, fierce and vigilant, they are always on the look out for trouble, and when they see it, they will charge it down and bite it. In fact, the warthog has a reputation for biting off way more than anyone thinks it can chew, facing down and even killing animals much larger, and allegedly more aggressive than itself when threatened. Warthogs are generally considered a great friend and a fierce enemy, who has no idea of their own ability, but are full of confidence, the sort of person who will agree to a project goal without thinking if it is possible, but who will then throw everything they have at trying to achieve it, no matter what.

Weasel characters are proper characters, always with a uniquely quirky personality, that are known for their playful nature, courage and quick wits. Weasels can also be known for their aggression, cunning and for playing innocent when they are anything but. Weasels are specialised hunters, that favour carnivorous snakes as their own pray, especially if the case of the Mongoose (which is really just a weasel cousin).

Whale characters are seen as peaceful and compassionate beings, although many are aggressive hunters, they are quite social creatures, although they have a reputation for solitude, they do communicate over vast distances of the oceans with their incredible songs. Whale characters are seen as intelligent, inventive, powerful and graceful, as well as enduring, but this majestic combination can lead to them seeming vain, proud or arrogant depending on their levels of charisma. The greatest strength the whales possess in their own opinion is their ability to navigate the lea, although they are not quite as gifted in that regard as Moles they are a close second.

Wolf characters are known as predators and deceivers, they may be charming and beautiful to look at, and display some characteristics of Dogs, being brave, and smart, even loyal for a time, if well "fed", but the Wolf has strong hungers, that are often dark and strange, and that almost always involve preying on others in some form or another. This hunger will drive the wolf to kill, in order to sate whatever need they feel.
